decks.notes: The primary focus of this deck is to quickly cycle through all of its cards and play a high number of spells in order to win the game with Thassa's Oracle. The deck includes a variety of cards that allow for quick mana generation and card draw, along with creatures that can assist in quickly milling through the library. The goal is to combo off and win the game in a single turn, often catching opponents off guard with its explosive plays.

The deck focuses on generating card advantage and mana acceleration through Aluren, allowing the player to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards like Acererak the Archlich and Atraxa, Grand Unifier, the deck can create a powerful board state that can easily win the game. With access to counterspells like Force of Will and protection like Veil of Summer, the deck can efficiently protect its key pieces while setting up its combo. The inclusion of Show and Tell and Stock Up provides alternative win conditions and additional card advantage.

This deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier using cards like Collective Brutality, Dark Ritual, and Lotus Petal. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Thoughtseize and Unmask while efficiently bringing back creatures from the graveyard with Reanimate, Shallow Grave, and Animate Dead. The deck's mana base includes fetch lands to ensure consistent access to the necessary colors of mana. Additionally, Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers and Cabal Therapy provide additional disruption and ways to interact with the opponent's hand and board.

This deck revolves around using various artifacts and combo pieces to generate powerful synergies and win conditions. Cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Goblin Welder help to recur and manipulate key pieces, while Urza's Saga provides additional ways to search for crucial artifacts. The inclusion of cards like Pinnacle Emissary and Phyrexian Dragon Engine offer alternative win conditions, adding flexibility to the combo strategy. By using a combination of card advantage, resource manipulation, and powerful artifact synergies, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with its powerful combos and versatile gameplay.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board using a variety of removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending, along with counterspells such as Force of Will and Force of Negation. The deck also aims to generate card advantage and manipulate draws with cards like Brainstorm and Ponder. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Murktide Regent provide additional card advantage and pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of Lórien Revealed allows for extra card draw and Snapcaster Mage offers versatility by allowing the player to recast spells from the graveyard.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently play all spells to combo off and win the game in a single turn. With an abundance of mana acceleration and ways to quickly fill the graveyard with creatures, the deck aims to use cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to mill the entire library and win with Thassa's Oracle or other finishers like Pact of Negation or Reanimate. The deck's strategy revolves around executing a fast and powerful combo to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to react.

This deck revolves around cheating powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn into play early through cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with these high-impact creatures while also having ways to protect the combo with counter spells like Force of Will. The deck also includes cards like Omniscience to further enhance the combo potential. The synergy between these cards allows for explosive plays and can quickly close out games before the opponent has a chance to respond.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's actions through counterspells, lands destruction, and removal spells, while also generating card advantage through card draw and filtering effects. It protects its game plan with disruption and answers to threats, aiming to out-value the opponent with powerful planeswalkers and efficient removal spells. By efficiently managing resources and dictating the pace of the game, the deck aims to grind the opponent out of resources and eventually win through incremental advantage.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and blue spells to create a fast-paced aggressive strategy. By maximizing the use of cards like Urza's Saga, Mox Opal, and Mishra's Bauble, the deck aims to quickly generate mana and card advantage. The inclusion of powerful creatures like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Thought Monitor further enhances the deck's ability to apply pressure and maintain board presence. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of aggressive artifact creatures and efficient blue spells.

The main concept of this deck is to control the board and generate mana through Gaea's Cradle while using creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Wight of the Reliquary to outvalue the opponent. The deck also utilizes cards like Natural Order and Green Sun's Zenith to search for key threats like Craterhoof Behemoth. Additionally, the deck includes disruptive elements like Collector Ouphe and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to be a versatile and powerful control deck that can adapt to different matchups effectively.

The deck focuses on using efficient creatures and disruptive spells to quickly apply pressure on the opponent while also controlling the board. It aims to combine aggressive plays with tempo-based strategies, utilizing cards like Barrowgoyf and Murktide Regent to continuously apply pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By utilizing a mix of card advantage, disruption, and efficient threats, the deck aims to stay ahead in the game and force the opponent into making suboptimal plays. The key strategy is to maintain control of the board while quickly closing out the game.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while maintaining a strong board presence. It utilizes a combination of hand disruption, removal spells, and efficient creatures to control the pace of the game. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Murktide Regent provide card advantage and threats, while Orcish Bowmasters and Barrowgoyf serve as aggressive creatures. The deck also has access to counterspells like Daze and Force of Will to protect its game plan. By applying pressure while disrupting the opponent, this deck aims to close out games quickly and efficiently.

The main focus of this UWx Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through countering spells, disrupting lands, and controlling the board using removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. It also aims to draw cards and create card advantage through cards like Ponder, Brainstorm, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. Quantum Riddler provides a win condition by taxing the opponent's resources, while Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd offers protection and a way to generate extra value. Overall, the deck aims to control the game and outvalue the opponent to secure victory.

The deck focuses on using powerful reanimation spells like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave to quickly bring back high-impact creatures such as Archon of Cruelty, Griselbrand, and Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Unmask, Thoughtseize, and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up the combo. The goal is to quickly assemble the necessary pieces to bring back a powerful creature and overwhelm the opponent with its abilities, ultimately leading to a quick victory.

This deck focuses on quickly playing powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield by using cards like Show and Tell, Sneak Attack, and Omniscience. It aims to disrupt opponents with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze while generating mana with cards like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb. The deck also includes card draw and manipulation with Brainstorm and Ponder to set up the combo and win the game quickly.

The deck focuses on quickly casting powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Archon of Cruelty by utilizing cards like Show and Tell, Sneak Attack, and Omniscience. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with these high-impact creatures early in the game, often bypassing traditional play sequences. The deck includes protection in the form of counterspells like Force of Will and disruption with cards like Flusterstorm. Additionally, mana acceleration cards like Lotus Petal, City of Traitors, and Ancient Tomb help to efficiently cast the expensive creatures.
